transcript.text: Music
Hey, she told you my name is Mike Wolf, and a little of my background, I've been in the
radio and television business in Washington, D.C., Denver, Colorado.
So I've traveled a bit and got tired of the big cities and came to Central Oregon.
What I see is very simply that you have some things to say that a lot of people
at this point don't buy, which you may not care about.
Why don't you care about it?
Why should I care?
But why shouldn't you care, and we can play Monopoly with this all the way through?
No.
You state that your interest is in the individual.
Is that the individual only who is here?
No.
The individual is everywhere, whether he is here or not, because only the individual exists.
Societies are just names, collectivities are all names.
The reality is only of the individual, and I'm trying my best, sitting in my room.
That's why you can see, on all the papers, in the radios, in the television, a man who
does not go out of this small valley, these and I will go on hammering all over the world
from here.
What is the point?
Well, let me ask you this, you do not go out of the valley, but is it not correct
that you spent three nights in Bend at the Riverhouse Motel a couple of weeks ago?
No.
Well, then someone is paying you the highest compliment because they are impersonating you.
And I wonder if it's possible for what you are saying, getting back to it somewhat seriously,
to be projected to those people who just don't understand?
First, the non-serious part.
Don't try to get a better laugh than I did.
I was not in the hotel, but I can be.
Well, you probably could buy the hotel if you wanted to.
In fact, I have.
And I have everything that any hotel in America can have.
Just to see my bathroom.
Perhaps it is the best in the whole world.
Your bathroom.
My bathroom.
Is that an invitation?
Yes.
I have never been invited to the bathroom.
I have been told I belonged in the bathroom before, but never invited.
I am a rare and unique person in every way.
I invite you in my bathroom.
That is unique.
But where do we go from there?
There is nowhere to go.
Enjoy the jacuzzi there.
Enjoy the sour there.
Enjoy the hot sour.
Enjoy the ice cold sour.
And then get out.
I understand.
I need to know this for my own edification.
Have you ever invited a member of the press to your bathroom before?
No.
Thank you.
I think that's important.
Because I never liked any man the way I like you.
I wonder if this could be love.
This is.
Invited to the bathroom.
I am sure the listeners of this program are thinking you and I are going to start a nightclub
act.
What is the potential, since you were talking about this a moment ago, of uniting the western
philosophy, which is somewhat materialistic, and the eastern philosophy, which traditionally
is not materialistic, but is more an inner, what would you call it, an inner recognition
of oneself.
Now you have done a magnificent job in many respects.
That diamond wrist watch, of course, is certainly not an eastern philosophy.
And yet the rest of your presentation is somewhat eastern.
Is it possible for those two cultures to unite?
That's what I have been doing.
And that's why both the sides of the culture are angry at me.
The easterns are angry with me because they think I am a materialist.
And are they right?
No.
You're not materialistic?
No, not in their sense.
And the western people, particularly the materialists, the communists, they are angry with me
because I am bringing spiritualism.
My approach is that man is both together.
This existence is not split into matter and spirit.
This existence is one organic unity.
Just as your soul and your body is an organic unity,
your soul cannot exist without your body
and your body cannot be alive without the soul.
I am destroying the whole idea of the separation
of a split between matter and mind, body and soul,
materialism and spiritualism.
I call myself Zorba di Buddha.
My restaurants, my discos are all named after me Zorba di Buddha.
Now Buddhists are angry.
The ambassador of Ceylon in America wrote me a letter
saying that in my country people will be angry about it.
Please don't use Buddha's name with Zorba.
I replied to him that we don't mean your Buddha.
Your Buddha's name was Gautam Siddhartha.
Buddha is not anybody's name.
It is a quality, it means the awakened one.
It can be used for anybody who is awakened.
So don't get upset about it.
My effort is that Zorba and Buddha have to be bridged.
Zorba represents the materialist life in all its luxury.
Hence this diamond wrist watch.
Hence all my are all slices.
I live the most luxurious life than any man had ever lived
with no worry.
People have much more money, I don't have a single cent.
But they cannot live such a luxurious life as I'm living
with no worry, no bank account, no income tax,
and having everything that anybody can have.
What is your secret?
I really think you're in the wrong business.
There are those.
Because of what you've just documented,
you must be one of the world's great con men, it would say.
And it takes a great man to admit that.
Certainly.
